title: blockram inference on ECP5 results in large amount of supporting logic
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.25+58 (git sha1 956c4e485a9, gcc 11.3.0 -fPIC -Os)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

WIth this verilog:

```
`timescale 100fs/100fs
module topEntity
    ( // Inputs
      input  clk // clock
    , input [7:0] r
    , input [24:0] wr

      // Outputs
    , output wire [15:0] result
    );
  reg [15:0] result_1;

  reg [15:0] result_1_RAM [0:256-1];

  always @(posedge clk) begin : result_1_blockRam
    if (wr[24:24]) begin
      result_1_RAM[wr[23:16]] <= wr[15:0];
    end
    result_1 <= result_1_RAM[r];
  end

  assign result = result_1;
endmodule
```

A ton of supporting logic is synthesized using the `synth_ecp5` command:

```
=== topEntity ===

   Number of wires:                 56
   Number of wire bits:            219
   Number of public wires:          56
   Number of public wire bits:     219
   Number of memories:               0
   Number of memory bits:            0
   Number of processes:              0
   Number of cells:                 93
     DP16KD                          1
     L6MUX21                         5
     LUT4                           37
     PFUMX                           8
     TRELLIS_FF                     42

``` 

### Expected Behavior

I expect it to basically only produce a blockram (DP16KD). In comparison `synth_xilinx` does synthesize to a single blockRAM(RAMB18E1) without any supporting logic:

```
=== topEntity ===

   Number of wires:                 15
   Number of wire bits:            281
   Number of public wires:           5
   Number of public wire bits:      66
   Number of memories:               0
   Number of memory bits:            0
   Number of processes:              0
   Number of cells:                 52
     BUFG                            1
     IBUF                           34
     OBUF                           16
     RAMB18E1                        1

   Estimated number of LCs:          0
```

### Actual Behavior

I expect `synth_ecp5` to behave similarly to `synth_xilinx`. I don't know the reason why all this extra logic needs to be introduced for dp16kd blockrams.
